title: After Iran war panic, U.S. scales back Middle East evacuations as demand drops

title: Generation Jihad | Day 12 of the Iran War
description:
After 12 days of war—and the death of Iran’s supreme leader—the Islamic Republic is under unprecedented pressure. Bill Roggio and Janatan Sayeh assess this initial phase of the war, the gap between Washington and Jerusalem’s goals, and the decisive question still looming over Tehran: will the Iranian people finish the job?

title: Banking, finance and consultancy firms evacuate staff in Gulf States
description: Several major international businesses closed or evacuated offices in Gulf countries after Iran threatened US- and Israel-linked economic targets and centres in the Middle East. US finance group Citi and British consultancy Deloitte evacuated offices in Dubai's financial centre, while PwC, another British consultancy, closed offices in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the UAE and Kuwait. HSBC has closed all branches in Qatar until further notice.

Despite its short life, the claim sent oil markets into a frenzy, sparked a denial from the White House and triggered mockery from Iranian officials. Vedika Bahl explains in Truth or Fake.

title: Iran escalates attacks on Kurdistan Region of Iraq
description:
Iran and Iranian-backed militias have escalated drone and missile attacks on the Kurdistan Region of Iraq, striking sites in Erbil and Sulaimani Governorates and killing at least three people. Many of the attacks are targeting Kurdish Iranian opposition groups.
The post Iran escalates attacks on Kurdistan Region of Iraq appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: Analysis: Israel strikes over 500 targets in Lebanon, issues evac orders in renewed war with Hezbollah (March 2–6)
description:
The November 27, 2024, ceasefire agreement in Lebanon has officially ended. Israel and Hezbollah are now reengaged in a full conflict after the group attacked Israeli territory on March 2. The Israelis have responded ferociously, targeting large swathes of Lebanon and promising that their campaign will continue until Hezbollah’s destruction. 
The post Analysis: Israel strikes over 500 targets in Lebanon, issues evac orders in renewed war with Hez

title: Analysis: Similarities and differences in US and Israeli targets and objectives in Iran campaign
description:
The US and Israel have cooperated to achieve impressive operational successes against Iran, but a publicly shared definition of victory remains elusive. This inconsistency and lack of clarity may negatively impact the allies’ ability to achieve durable strategic objectives.
The post Analysis: Similarities and differences in US and Israeli targets and objectives in Iran campaign appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: Iran and proxy militias strike energy infrastructure, US bases, and Gulf capitals on March 6
description:
On March 6, Iran and its aligned militias continued their campaign of missile and drone attacks across the Middle East, targeting civilian infrastructure, military installations, airports, and political sites in Bahrain, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the UAE.
The post Iran and proxy militias strike energy infrastructure, US bases, and Gulf capitals on March 6 appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: Israeli strike eliminates Hamas commander in Lebanon
description:
The Israeli military killed Hamas commander Wasim Atallah Ali in Tripoli on March 5. The IDF said that Ali was responsible for training the group’s branch in Lebanon. His elimination adds to the growing number of Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad members who have been killed in Lebanon by the Israeli military. 
The post Israeli strike eliminates Hamas commander in Lebanon appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: US and Israel establish air superiority, focus strikes on Iranian military sites and ‘regime pillars’ (March 4–5)
description:
On March 4 and 5, the United States and Israel continued attacks against elements of the Islamic Republic’s military assets, including its missile and naval threat capacity, as well as its repression apparatus. The countries also declared air superiority over Iran and signaled the beginning of a new phase of the operation.
The post US and Israel establish air superiority, focus strikes on Iranian military sites and regime pillars (March 4–5) appe

title: Iran war may force US to shift missile defenses from South Korea, Seoul says
description: The U.S. Army operates only a small number of THAAD batteries across the world, meaning a deployment can affect multiple theaters.

title: US, Iran spar over status of Iranian warship sunk by submarine
description: The United States and Iran have offered sharply different accounts of the sinking of an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean last week.

title: US B-1B Lancers arrive at RAF Fairford as strikes on Iran intensify
description: The U.K. Ministry of Defence confirmed Saturday that U.S. forces had begun using the British base for “specific defensive operations."

title: Prewar US intel found intervention in Iran wasn’t likely to change leadership
description: A February intelligence assessment determined that American military intervention was not likely to lead to regime change in the Islamic Republic.

title: US has destroyed entire class of Iranian warships, CENTCOM commander says
description: The U.S. had struck more than 60 Iranian ships, according to U.S. Central Command Commander Adm. Brad Cooper.

title: Trump to join grieving families for return of soldiers killed in Iran war
description: The president is joining families at Dover Air Force Base at the dignified transfer for the six U.S. troops killed in the war in the Middle East.

title: US to send anti-drone system to Mideast after successful use in Ukraine, officials say
description: The system that is being sent, known as Merops, flies drones against drones. It is small enough to fit in the back of a midsize pickup truck.

title: The race for Paris: How the capital’s housing crisis could determine the city’s next mayor
description: With France preparing to vote in the first round of the municipal elections on Sunday, Paris has become a battleground between left- and right-wing forces fighting to shape the capital’s future. FRANCE 24 looks at how the city’s affordable housing crisis is shaping the tight-run race.

title: Trump a lot more concern about how war and oil prices will affect midterms
description: Since the war has started, fourteen ships have been hit in the Strait of Hormuz, as Iran vows to carry out an 'oil blockade'. Although oil prices have gone down after sharply increasing, concern remains. French President Emmanuel Macron is calling on G7 leaders to work together to reestablish 'freedom of navigation' in the region as soon as possible. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle looks at the politics of this crisis, and the US strategy.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic down 97% as fourteen ships struck since war started
description: A British monitoring agency says three ships in the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz were struck in separate incidents on March 11, a day after several US media reported Iran had started laying mines in the strategic passagepoint. The UN meanwhile has urged for 'humanitarian exemptions' to get aid through the Strait.

title: Centre of Beirut shocked after Israeli army strikes apartment block
description: Over 700,000 people have been displaced in Lebanon amid intense Israeli strikes, with nearly 600 having died since the start of the war. Although the capital has mostly been spared, it has suffered two strikes in its heart. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris Trent speaks from where the latest bomb struck.

title: IEA to release record amount of emergency oil reserves as Middle East war rattles markets
description: The International Energy Agency (IEA) said its 32 members would release a record 400 million barrels from strategic reserves to steady markets as the Middle East war continue to push oil prices higher and threaten global supply.

title: 'We have been betrayed': Nagorno-Karabakh ex-minister on Armenia-Azerbaijan peace deal
description: FRANCE 24 spoke to Artak Beglaryan, former state minister and human rights ombudsman of Nagorno-Karabakh. The peace declaration signed between Armenia and Azerbaijan last August in Washington raised cautious hopes, but for displaced residents of Nagorno-Karabakh the situation remains difficult. “It’s relatively good news,” Beglaryan said, “but nothing was written in this declaration about Nagorno-Karabakh and the people of Nagorno-Karabakh.”

title: US likely responsible for deadly Iran school strike after targeting error, report says
description: The New York Times on Wednesday reported that a US Tomahawk missile likely hit a school in Iran due to outdated targeting data, killing more than 150 people. This comes after US President Donald Trump suggested that Iran itself might have been responsible for the strike.

title: G7 releases record amount of oil stocks to 'calm the markets down'
description: G7 leaders have announced the record release of 400 million barrels of oil in response to the war in the Middle East. This represents about twenty days worth of usual oil traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, currently through dangerous to go through due to the threat of Iranian strikes. This initiative aims 'to calm markets down', as FRANCE 24's Philip Turle explains.

title: Strait of Hormuz: US says it destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels
description: The US said it took out more than a dozen Iranian mine-laying vessels Tuesday as the Islamic Republic vowed to block the region's oil exports, saying it would not allow “even a single litre” to be shipped to its enemies.

title: Beirut: FRANCE 24 in the heart of the bombed southern suburbs
description: Our special correspondents in Beirut were able to visit the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital, which have been under Israeli bombardments for a week.At least 500 people have been killed in Lebanon since the start of the war in the Middle East and some 700,000 others have been forced to flee, according to the latest figures. But some residents remain in the area despite the danger.

title: Bodies of anonymous migrants wash up in Sicily weeks after cyclone
description: Over the past weeks, the bodies of anonymous migrants have washed up on the shores of Sicily, victims of the recent extreme weather caused by Storm Harry as they were attempting to cross the sea from north Africa. The cyclone has underlined a brutal reality: that the central Mediterranean remains one of the most dangerous migratory routes in the world. FRANCE 24's Natalia Mendoza tells us more.

title: Volatile oil markets due to Middle East conflict increase jet fuel prices
description: Volatility on oil markets is already being felt in the travel sector, as a spike in the price of jet fuel is being passed on to air passengers. Higher fares and fuel surcharges are beginning to appear across a number of airlines. With analysts warning that the war on Iran is likely to weigh on the travel sector for the year to come.

title: 'King of the World' statue shows Trump with Epstein in "Titanic" recreation
description: A new art installation by the anonymous artist collective Secret Handshake appeared this week on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., depicting figures representing US President Donald Trump and the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The installation, titled "King of the World," portrays the two figures in a recreation of an iconic scene from the film Titanic featuring characters Jack and Rose.

title: 'US war on Iran tactically very successful: Strategic success is going to be elusive'
description: Genie Godula welcomes Daniel Schneiderman, Director of Global Policy Programs at Penn Washington. He argues that while the US has achieved significant tactical military successes in its campaign against Iran, the battlefield gains do not necessarily translate into victory. Washington’s stated objectives shift, day to day, from destroying Iran’s military and nuclear capabilities all the way to regime change. While Iran is focused on survival: the

title: Bangladesh demolish Pakistan who suffer record low score in contest
description: Pakistan bowled out for the lowest total in a match against Bangladesh as the latter claim 8-wicket win to open series.

title: IEA recommends record 400M barrel oil release amid Iran war
description: The International Energy Agency has agreed to release a record 400 million barrels of oil.

title: UN warns of widening crisis as Israeli attacks displace 750,000 in Lebanon
description: UN humanitarian chief says mass displacement accelerating as Lebanons shelters are already overcrowded, lack resources.

title: Not ‘a litre of oil’ to pass Strait of Hormuz, expect $200 price tag: Iran
description: Warning comes as 400 million barrels of oil are being released from global reserves during waterways closure.

title: US military confirms use of ‘advanced AI tools’ in war against Iran
description: Admiral Brad Cooper says artificial intelligence is helping process data, but humans are making final decisions.

title: Could the US deploy troops to Iran, and how could that play out?
description: Experts say Iran’s vast, mountainous terrain would make invasion difficult but a small, precise mission is possible.

title: The Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire was built to fail
description: Israeli attacks never stopped, and the mechanism meant to police violations had no power to stop them.

title: Sudan’s devastating war rages on as regional rivalries deepen
description: Yet the fallout from the Iran war could push Gulf rivals to cooperate, opening space for diplomacy on Sudan.

title: IEA agrees to release 400 million barrels of oil from strategic reserves
description: Proposed release is larger than the 182 million barrels of oil that IEA ​members released after Russia invaded Ukraine.

title: China’s Military Has Quietly Cut Flying Near Taiwan
description: For years, China has flown military jets near Taiwan almost daily. Then they suddenly stopped, leaving analysts to wonder why.

title: Bowen: Trump has called for an Iran uprising but the lessons from Iraq in 1991 loom large
description: The US president might learn that starting wars is much easier than ending them, writes the BBC's international editor.

title: 'Even under missiles we carry on living' - how young Iranians are coping with war
description: Iranians say they are sheltering at home and rarely venturing out on near-empty streets as the US-Israeli bombing campaign continues.

title: LIVE: Real Madrid vs Manchester City – Champions League last 16
description: Follow the build-up, analysis and live text commentary of the game as Madrid host City for last 16, first leg showdown.

title: Iran holds a public mourning ceremony for commanders killed by strikes.
description: Thousands of people mourned in a state-sanctioned ceremony, as people in Tehran described deepening anxiety and fear as the war continued.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Coalition of 32 Countries to Tap Oil Reserves to Keep Prices in Check
description: The Strait of Hormuz, a conduit for a fifth of the world’s oil, is all but closed as war in the Middle East expands. A British agency said three ships were hit by unidentified projectiles in or near the strait.

title: NYT сообщила об ущербе минимум 17 военным объектам США из-за ударов Ирана
description: Удары Ирана ракетами и беспилотниками привели к повреждениям, как минимум, на 17 американских военных базах и других объектах на Ближнем Востоке, сообщила The New York Times со ссылкой на спутниковые снимки и видеозаписи, подлинность которых удалось установить.

title: NYT: 17 военных объектов США на Ближнем Востоке были атакованы Ираном
description: В ходе иранских атак по американским военным объектам на Ближнем Востоке как минимум 17 из них получили повреждения. Об этом сообщает газета The New York Times (NYT) со ссылкой на спутниковые снимки, видеозаписи в соцсетях, иранские медиа и заявления официальных лиц США.

title: Trump tells Axios there's "practically nothing left" to target in Iran
description: President Trump told Axios in a brief phone interview Wednesday that the war with Iran will end "soon" because there is "practically nothing left to target.""Little this and that... Any time I want it to end, it will end," Trump said during the five-minute call.  Why it matters: Even as Trump publicly signals his operation has largely accomplished its objectives, U.S. and Israeli officials say there has been no internal directive on when fighting

title: Gas price spikes are slamming Senate battleground states
description: The Iran war's fuel-price shock is slamming states that could decide Senate control in November, a potential headache for Republicans defending their majority.Why it matters: Affordability was already Democrats' central midterm message, and now the cost of President Trump's unpopular war is on display at the pump.Just 29% of Americans approve of the strikes, and two-thirds — including 44% of Republicans — expect gas prices to keep rising, accordi

title: International agency calls for historic oil release amid Iran war
description: The International Energy Agency said Wednesday its member governments will jointly release up to 400 million barrels of oil from strategic stockpiles after the Iran war set off a chaotic spike in crude prices.Why it matters: It would be the largest joint release in the history of IEA, which coordinates members' emergency responses to oil shocks. The effort is the most aggressive energy policy response yet to the conflict that's bottling up massiv

title: Ukraine will help combat Iranian drones in Middle East, Zelenskyy says
description:
Ukraine says it will help the United States and its Middle East allies combat the Iranian drones that the Tehran regime is firing at the region. Russia has bombarded Ukrainian cities with these weapons for years, resulting in a series of defensive adaptations and innovations by the Armed Forces of Ukraine¬—and unmatched counter-drone experience.
The post Ukraine will help combat Iranian drones in Middle East, Zelenskyy says appeared first on FDDs

title: Iran targets US bases, diplomatic missions, and oil infrastructure in Arab countries on March 3–5
description:
Between March 3 and March 5, Iran continued retaliating against the joint United States-Israeli military campaign by conducting widespread strikes across Arab Gulf countries using ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and drones. Attacks were launched against Jordan, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, and the United Arab Emirates.

title: Amid US military actions, White House struggles to explain how Iran war will end
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Tuesday told reporters it’s up to Trump “whether it’s the beginning, the middle or the end” of the war.

title: Two Iranian warships take sanctuary in India and Sri Lanka
description: The sinking of an Iranian warship by a U.S. Navy submarine last week became a diplomatic embarrassment for New Delhi.

title: NATO to study what-if scenarios that could cause Arctic conflict with Russia
description: The alliance's biennial Cold Response drill will take place from March 9-19 across Norway.

title: Russia provided Iran with information that can help Tehran strike US military, sources say
description: The information could help Tehran strike American warships, aircraft and other assets in the region.

title: No deal with Iran except ‘unconditional surrender,’ Trump says
description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump wrote that “IRAN WILL HAVE A GREAT FUTURE” should the Islamic Republic decide to put down its arms.

title: Paris mayoral race: Can the left hold on to the French capital?
description: Paris gears up to turn the page after 12-years of Anne Hidalgo. Can the French Socialist party retain control of City Hall after ruling the city for 25 years? Find out who the main contenders in the race are in this edition of Entre Nous.

title: Six members of Iranian women's football team granted asylum in Australia
description: The Iranian women’s soccer team left Australia without six squad members after frantic final efforts inside the terminal by Australian officials, who sought to ensure the women understood they were being offered asylum. Initially offered to seven, one of the players changed her mind in a last-minute decision after speaking to other squad members.

title: Libyan Ramadan celebrations tempered by rising inflation
description: Rising inflation has dampened Ramadan celebrations in Libya. Across the country, families have had to cope with higher prices. In the eastern city of Benghazi, residents have embraced the spirit of the holy month by lending a helping hand to the city's most disadvantaged people. FRANCE 24's Lilia Blaise and Hamdi Tlili report.

title: New Iranian supreme leader 'safe and sound' amid rumours surrounding wellbeing
description: The son of Iran's president has said the new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is "safe and sound" following reports the late ayatollah's son was injured in a strike that killed his father and his wife on the first day of the war. Khamenei has not been seen since his nomination, sparking rumours regarding his wellbeing.

title: ‘The repression machine is still deployed’: Exclusive testimony from a Tehran resident
description: FRANCE 24 has gathered rare testimonies from residents of Tehran, directly affected by US-Israeli strikes since February 28. Vahid, an opponent of the regime whose identity has been concealed for security reasons, describes daily life in the Iranian capital. A supporter of military intervention, he also shares his doubts and fears about the future.

title: Lebanese displaced by Israeli strikes speak to FRANCE 24
description: For the past ten days, Israel has been relentlessly bombing Hezbollah strongholds in Beirut’s southern suburbs as well as southern Lebanon. As the number of displaced people now approaches 760,000, some have found shelter in reception centres in the capital, while others are forced to sleep on the streets. Our special correspondents spoke to several of them.

title: While southern Lebanon evacuates, northern Israel stays put
description: France will be sending 60 tons of humanitarian aid to Lebanon, as entire towns in the south of the country are under evacuation after orders were issued by the Israeli army. But across the border in Israel the strategy is different and many choose to stay put. The government is determined to keep people in their homes after it spent billions housing hundreds of thousands of displaced following the October 7 attacks.

title: Lebanon grapples with death, displacement and widening Hezbollah-Israel conflict
description: With 570 people killed and more than 750,000 displaced amid fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, Lebanon finds itself in what Iva Kovic-Chahine, head of the Beirut-based news outlet L’Orient Today, describes as an “absurd” situation. She tells FRANCE 24 that many Lebanese felt “intense rage” after the Iran-backed group fired rockets into Israel to protest the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The Lebanese government faces growing pressure to e

title: Russia Says Ukraine’s Strike on Factory City Was Deadly
description: Ukraine said the attack struck a key electronics plant, highlighting how it remains capable of hitting high-value targets inside Russia.

title: Countries agree to record release of emergency oil reserves as prices surge
description: The G7 group of nations welcomes the idea of releasing oil in response to the surge in prices since the US-Israel war with Iran began

title: EU and UK demand Israel stop surge in West Bank settler violence since Iran war
description: Six Palestinians have been killed during attacks by settlers in the West Bank since the start of Israel's war, the UN says.

title: Ukraine says it has hit Russian 'missile component' plant
description: Russia says the attack hit civilians and could not have been carried out without British help.

title: Mugabe's son drops bail request - what has happened to the family after losing power
description: Bellarmine Mugabe will negotiate a plea deal after being accused of attempted murder and other charges in South Africa.

title: What happened in Ukraine missile attack in Russia’s Bryansk?
description: A Ukrainian missile attack on Russia’s Bryansk killed 6 civilians and injured 37 others, according to local officials.

title: Iran cannot participate in the FIFA World Cup, sports minister says
description: Iran says under no circumstances will it participate following the US-Israeli attacks that killed its supreme leader.

title: Funeral held for top Iranian commanders killed in US-Israeli strikes
description: Thousands of people gathered in Tehran to mourn top Iranian military officials killed by US-Israeli strikes.

description: Iraq has seen attacks from both sides of the conflict: Iran and its proxies on the one side and the US on the other.

description: Turkiye’s president has urged a return to the negotiating table before the Iran war “sets the entire region on fire.”

description: Intelligence experts have suggested China has made its satellite navigation system available to Iran.

description: Returning to Russian fossil fuels would be a “strategic blunder” says European Commission president Ursula von der Leyen

title: South African soldiers deploy in Johannesburg to tackle crime and gangs
description: First troops touch down nearly a month after President Ramaphosa said organised crime threatened countrys democracy.

description: Madrid recalls ambassador amid rising diplomatic tensions and criticism of US-Israeli actions in Iran.

description: The police requested the ban citing public disorder risks, while the organisers decide to hold a static protest instead.

description: Analyst refers to security achievement gap of tactical assassinations that fail to bring Israel security.

description: Al Jazeera visited the first women-only mosque in the Middle East, in the Qatari capital Doha.

description: As Kyiv liberates parts of Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhia, Zelenskyy says Russia losing up to 35,000 soldiers a month.

title: Generation Jihad | We didn’t light it, but we tried to fight it
description:
Many Americans believed a conflict with the Islamic Republic would be a new war.
But as Bill Roggio and Behnam Ben Taleblu explain, the truth is simpler: this war began in 1979. Now, the question isn’t how the war started. It’s how it ends. Is this a limited war to degrade the Tehran regime — or the beginning of the end for the Islamic Republic?
The post Generation Jihad | We didnt light it, but we tried to fight it appeared first on FDDs Long Wa

title: Australia deploys early-warning aircraft to the Middle East amid Iran attacks
description: Australian leaders have said the country won’t be a protagonist in Israel and U.S. combat operations.

title: Iran to face ‘most intense day of strikes,’ Hegseth says
description: The rhetoric was equally sharp from Tehran. Iran’s parliament speaker said on X that Iran was “definitely not looking for a ceasefire."

title: Diego Garcia base access: Getting past the misinformation
description: There have been many twists and turns over the years in this dispute, including Trump’s differences with Starmer over Greenland and Iran.

title: Australian submariners have a brush with Iran war
description: “It would be quite straightforward to not be on the consoles or in the control room" during a recent U.S. submarine torpedo kill, said one analyst.

title: Central Beirut targeted by Israeli strikes for second time since war started
description: An Israeli strike hit an apartment block in central Beirut on March 11, further widening Israeli attacks in the capital beyond the Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs where ​heavy bombardment continued. Israeli strikes have killed nearly 600 people in Lebanon, and uprooted 700,000 more, according to Lebanese authorities.

title: "No sign of Iranian uprising" despite US-Israeli encouragement
description: Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei is injured but "safe and sound", the son of the president said on March 11, offering the first official explanation for why the 56-year-old has not been seen since his appointment. Meanwhile, the US and Israel are hoping to see an uprising in Iran which would dislodge the regime - to no avail. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley looks into the political situation in the country.

title: Tehran vows to keep blockade on regional oil flow as it targets Gulf states
description: More than 2,000 Iranian drones have been fired at Gulf states since the start of the war in the Middle East on February 28, with Tehran targeting US allies in the Gulf. The Iranian regime claims to have decimated the US military in the region, while the US says it has destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels.

title: DRC youth disillusioned by politics ahead of presidential elections
description: The DRC is preparing to elect its next president. But some young voters in the African country are increasingly disillusioned with politics. Incumbent leader Denis Sassou-Nguesso, who is 82 years old, is the favorite to win after more than forty years in power.

title: Ukraine sending drone experts to three Gulf countries
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said that 11 countries had asked Kyiv for help in how to counter Iranian drones being fired by Tehran in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes. In return, Ukraine is sending drone experts to three countries - with the hope this will prove to be useful leverage for its fight against Russia back home.

title: Dubai airport and Saudi oil field among targets of Iranian drones
description: Israel has warned of attacks as Iranian counterstrikes continue against it and US allies in the Gulf. Saudi Arabia said it has neutralised five drones targeting one of the kingdom's oil field, while drones also fell near Dubai's airport.

title: Not "unlikely" some Iranian football players pressured to return from Australia
description: Australian police helped two more members of the Iranian women's soccer delegation slip their minders to claim asylum, but one has changed her mind and decided to go back to Iran, according to authorities. Concerns about the players' safety upon their return home ‌grew after Iranian state television labelled the team "wartime traitors" for refusing to sing the national anthem during a women's Asian ⁠Cup match in Australia. FRANCE 24's Grégory Ple

title: Iran adoption a strategy of 'war of attrition on several fronts'
description: On the second week of the war in the Middle East, Iran continues to target Israel and US allies in the Gulf. More strikes have been intercepted, according to reports across several countries. Iran's targets include oil infrastructures, which is part of its "war of attrition" strategy, which applies on several fronts. FRANCE 24's Hoda Abdel-Hamid tells us more from Doha, in Qatar.

title: Israel strikes central Beirut, as number of displaced goes over 700,000
description: Central Beirut was targeted by fresh Israeli strikes in the early hours of March 11, for the second time since the star of the war. More than 560 people have been killed and over 700,000 displaced - about a tenth of the population - since Israeli started striking the country. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris Trent has more from the ground.

title: Oil markets swing on stockpile release and Hormuz Strait mine reports
description: Oil markets fell and picked up again on Wednesday, as the International Energy Agency reportedly considers its largest-ever release of emergency crude stockpiles to stabilise global markets, while Iran is said to have laid naval mines in the Hormuz Strait. In this edition, we look at how the latest developments in the ongoing Middle East war are affecting markets.

title: Concern grows over possibility of Iran mining Strait of Hormuz
description: The US says it has destroyed over a dozen Iranian ships capable of laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. The crucial passage point for oil has been effectively closed due to Iranian threats. Trump warned Iran that the presence of mines would lead to military consequences "at a level never seen before".

title: US-Israeli bombing destroyed nearly 10,000 civilian sites, says Iranian UN envoy
description: Tehran was targeted by heavy bombardments on March 11 as the US-Israeli military campaign continued. The day before, US Secretary Pete Hegseth had warned that Iran would know the 'most intense day' of US strikes. Nearly 10,000 civilian sites have been destroyed since the war started, according to Iran's U.N. envoy.

title: India’s Supreme Court allows first-ever passive euthanasia death
description: Withdrawal of life support granted to family of 32-year-old man who has been in vegetative state for more than 12 years.

title: Iran’s strategic patience tactic failed, what comes next could be far worse
description: Restraint didnt prevent war; new Iranian leadership may now look to much more dangerous approach to build a deterrent.

title: 4 day week, fewer car trips in Philippines as Iran fallout bites
description: Rising fuel prices are pushing more people to use public transport and prompting a four-day work week in Manila.

title: Iranian female footballer changes mind on Australian asylum offer
description: One member of the Iranian women’s football team offered asylum by Australia has decided to return home to Iran.

title: Iran declares US-Israeli economic, banking interests in region are targets
description: IRGC releases a list of offices run by top US companies with Israeli links whose technology has been used militarily.

title: Iran reports hospitals, civilians affected during war with US, Israel
description: The Iranian Red Crescent Society says nearly 20,000 civilian buildings affected, along with 77 healthcare facilities.

title: Iran welcome to compete in FIFA World Cup, Trump tells Infantino
description: FIFAs Infantino says US president reiterates that the Iranian team is welcome to participate in the tournament.

title: A Trump-Xi Summit Nears, but China Doesn’t Know What Trump Wants
description: Trump’s agenda for the high-stakes meeting remains unclear to Beijing, Chinese analysts say, while American executives say they haven’t been invited along.

title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Was Wounded Early in the War, Iranian and Israeli Officials Say
description: Officials say Mojtaba Khamenei’s legs were hurt, but the circumstances as well as the extent of his injuries were unclear. He has remained out of view since being announced as leader three days ago.

title: World Heritage Sites Hit in Airstrikes on Iran
description: Revered cultural icons that have withstood the upheavals of history are being hit hard in the war being waged by Israel and the United States.

title: China’s Military Has Quietly Cut Flying Near Taiwan. But Why?
description: For years, China has flown military jets near Taiwan almost daily. Then they suddenly stopped, leaving analysts to wonder why.

title: Ukraine Says It Hit Key Russian Electronics Plant; Russia Reports Deaths
description: The attack highlighted how Ukraine remains capable of hitting high-value targets inside Russia.

title: Ukraine Can Now Manufacture ‘China-Free’ Drones
description: The country has prioritized self-sufficiency in producing a crucial battlefield weapon, though weaning itself fully off cheaper Chinese components is difficult.

title: Why Did the UK Police Repeatedly Decline to Investigate Claims About Epstein and Prince Andrew?
description: The police in London interviewed Virginia Giuffre three times over her allegations about Jeffrey Epstein, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Ghislaine Maxwell, but never began a criminal investigation.

title: Live Updates: At Least 3 Ships Come Under Attack Near Key Oil Passage
description: The Strait of Hormuz, a conduit for a fifth of the world’s oil, is all but closed as war in the Middle East expands. A British agency said three ships were hit by unidentified projectiles in or near the strait.

title: BZ: в ходе разговора с Путиным Трамп мог осознать свою ошибку по Ирану
description: Во время разговора с российским лидером Владимиром Путиным президент США Дональд Трамп, вероятно, осознал ошибку из-за роста цен на нефть после атак на Иран. Об этом пишет издание Berliner Zeitung. Читать далее

title: IDF targets Hezbollah command centers, weapons sites in strikes on Beirut suburbs
description:
Four said killed in IDF strike on central Beirut apartment * No injuries or direct impacts reported in overnight Iranian missile attacks * Four injured as drones downed at Dubai airport * UK police ban pro-Iranian London march due to 'extreme tensions'
The post IDF targets Hezbollah command centers, weapons sites in strikes on Beirut suburbs appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: OpenAI, Anthropic feud could prop up Google
description: Google is quietly expanding its Pentagon work — and growing users faster than its rivals — while Anthropic and OpenAI publicly spar over conditions for Defense Department work.Why it matters: Winning the AI race may depend on knowing when to stay out of scuffles.What they're saying: "When you have two major companies fighting each other, it is a good distraction for another company to come in and learn from their mistakes and just go all the way
